title: valeriana officinalis f. incisa
intro: Valeriana officinalis f. incisa is a subspecies of the valerian plant, known for its distinctive cut or incised leaves. It is a perennial flowering plant native to Europe and Asia, commonly used for its medicinal properties. The roots of Valeriana officinalis f. incisa contain valerenic acid, valeranone, and other compounds, which have a sedative and calming effect on the nervous system, making it a popular remedy for anxiety, insomnia, and restlessness. Valerian root preparations, including teas, tinctures, and capsules, can be used to promote relaxation, improve sleep quality, and alleviate symptoms of depression and stress.
